dataGuardian.faq1Q: What is the D-Secure Assurance Badge?
dataGuardian.faq1A: The D-Secure Assurance Badge is our certification that confirms your organization has implemented proper data hygiene processes. It's not just a certificate - it's our guarantee that you have the complete setup, processes, and discipline to keep data safe and secure.
dataGuardian.faq2Q: How long does the certification process take?
dataGuardian.faq2A: The typical certification process takes 2-4 weeks, depending on your organization's size and readiness. This includes assessment, implementation support, and final verification.
dataGuardian.faq3Q: What are the prerequisites for certification?
dataGuardian.faq3A: Organizations must implement D-Secure data hygiene tools including our automated eraser system, Free Space Wipe. We provide complete guidance on setup and implementation.
dataGuardian.faq4A: We conduct both scheduled and random audits to verify compliance. This includes checking erasure logs, process documentation, and system configurations. Our automated tools make this process seamless and non-intrusive.
dataGuardian.faq5A: We provide continuous support including regular software updates, technical assistance, compliance monitoring, and renewal coordination. Think of us as your ongoing partner in data hygiene.

manual.aboutDesc1: D-Secure File Eraser is a complete solution to maintain your computer privacy by erasing unwanted information from the computer. Erased data is beyond recovery. D-Secure File Eraser can be used to erase files/folders, Unused space, and System traces.
manual.aboutDesc2: D-Secure File Eraser erases files & folders completely from the hard drive. You can select multiple files/folders at a time for erasure. Once files/folders from the drive are erased using D-Secure File Eraser data can't be recovered back. The software allows you to generate and save the reports of the completed erasure process.
manual.aboutDesc3: In addition, it can erase unused space completely such that all the traces of previously stored data are completely removed. When you delete data from a hard drive, the data content is not deleted entirely, instead the space occupied by the data is marked as unused space and the new data is written on that unused space.
manual.aboutDesc4: D-Secure File Eraser also erases all the system traces. Operating systems store records of all activities such as browsing Internet and opening documents constantly.
manual.aboutDesc5: D-Secure File Eraser has a set of 20+ different erasure algorithms, that are accepted globally, to ensure permanent data deletion. You can choose any of the algorithms for erasure process. The software is menu driven, simple to use with an intuitive interface, and requires no prior technical skill.

manual.faq2A: No, once data is erased using D-Secure File Eraser with any of the supported algorithms, the data is permanently and irrecoverably destroyed.
manual.faq3A: D-Secure File Eraser supports Windows 10/11, macOS 10.15+, and major Linux distributions.
manual.faq4Q: How many erasure algorithms are available?
manual.faq4A: The software includes 20+ internationally accepted erasure algorithms including DoD 5220.22-M, NIST 800-88, Gutmann, and others.

manual.faq7A: Yes, the Scheduler feature allows you to set up automated erasure tasks with customizable frequency (once, daily, weekly, monthly) and timing.
manual.faq8Q: Does the software support multiple themes?
manual.faq8A: Yes, you can switch between light and dark mode themes for comfortable viewing.

manual.about_p1: D-Secure File Eraser is a complete solution to maintain your computer privacy by erasing unwanted information from the computer. Erased data is beyond recovery. D-Secure File Eraser can be used to erase files/folders, Unused space, and System traces.
manual.about_p2: D-Secure File Eraser erases files & folders completely from the hard drive. You can select multiple files/folders at a time for erasure. Once files/folders from the drive are erased using D-Secure File Eraser data can't be recovered back. The software allows you to generate and save the reports of the completed erasure process.
manual.about_p3: In addition, it can erase unused space completely such that all the traces of previously stored data are completely removed. When you delete data from a hard drive, the data content is not deleted entirely, instead the space occupied by the data is marked as unused space and the new data is written on that unused space.
manual.about_p4: D-Secure File Eraser also erases all the system traces. Operating systems store records of all activities such as browsing Internet and opening documents constantly.
manual.about_p5: D-Secure File Eraser has a set of 20+ different erasure algorithms, that are accepted globally, to ensure permanent data deletion. You can choose any of the algorithms for erasure process. The software is menu driven, simple to use with an intuitive interface, and requires no prior technical skill.
manual.feature_os_desc: - Compatible with Windows 10/11, macOS 10.15+, and Linux distributions.
manual.feature_erase_files_desc: - Option to erase selected files or folders from the hard drive.
manual.feature_free_space_desc: - Option to erase the free space to secure privacy from the selected volumes.
manual.feature_schedule_desc: - Supports schedule erasing tasks to run them automatically at specified periods.
manual.feature_reports_desc: - Option to generate and save 100% secure and tamper-proof erasure reports in various formats such as PDF
manual.feature_report_settings_desc: - Option to customise the reports before saving the report.
manual.feature_search_report_desc: - Option to search reports by selecting the appropriate date range or erasure type.
manual.feature_preview_desc: - Option to preview the report before saving.
manual.feature_cloud_desc: - Automatic report uploaded to D-Secure Cloud Console.
manual.feature_standards_desc: - Provides 20+ different erasing algorithms with 3 verification methods to erase the data permanently.
manual.feature_search_erase_desc: - Option to search specific files using name or extension on the system and erase them using a suitable algorithm.
manual.feature_themes_desc: - Option to switch between light and dark modes.
manual.guide_p1: This user guide contains steps to assist you in various functions of D-Secure File Eraser. Each function is explained in detail, in the corresponding sections. This guide covers the following major topics:
manual.guide_p2: This guide has the following features for easy navigation and understanding:
manual.guide_feature1: Select a topic from the list of topics given on the left of the navigation pane.
manual.guide_feature2_1: There are Notes and Tips in some topics of this guide for better understanding and ease of work. These Notes and Tips are given in italic style.
manual.guide_p3: This is a web-based guide and requires an active internet connection. You can read this guide with all popular browsers like Chrome, Firefox, Internet Explorer, and Safari. For better viewing experience, it is recommended to use the Chrome browser.
manual.install_intro: Follow these comprehensive steps to successfully install D-Secure File Eraser on your system:
manual.install_step1_title: 1. Download the installer from the official D-Secure website:
manual.install_step1_desc: Navigate to the official D-Secure website using your preferred web browser. Locate the Downloads section and select the appropriate installer version compatible with your operating system (Windows, macOS, or Linux). Ensure you download from the official source to avoid any security risks or corrupted files. The installer file will be saved to your default Downloads folder.
manual.install_step2_title: 2. Run the installer with administrator privileges:
manual.install_step2_desc: Once the download is complete, navigate to your Downloads folder and locate the D-Secure File Eraser installer file. Right-click on the installer executable and select "Run as Administrator" to ensure the installation process has the necessary permissions to install system-level components. This step is crucial for proper software integration with your operating system.
manual.install_step3_title: 3. Follow the installation wizard and accept the license agreement:
manual.install_step3_desc: The installation wizard will launch, presenting you with a welcome screen. Click "Next" to proceed through the setup process. Carefully read through the End User License Agreement (EULA) to understand the terms and conditions of use. Once reviewed, check the "I accept the agreement" checkbox and click "Next" to continue with the installation.
manual.install_step4_desc: The installer will prompt you to select a destination folder where D-Secure File Eraser will be installed. You can either accept the default installation path (typically C:\Program Files\D-Secure File Eraser on Windows) or click "Browse" to choose a custom location on your hard drive. Ensure the selected drive has sufficient free space for the installation, typically requiring at least 100-200 MB of available disk space.
manual.install_step5_desc: Review your installation settings on the summary screen to ensure all configurations are correct. Click "Install" to begin the actual installation process. The installer will copy all necessary files to your system, register components, and create shortcuts. Once the progress bar reaches 100%, click "Finish" to complete the installation. You may be prompted to restart your computer for all changes to take effect. The application will now be available in your Start Menu or Applications folder, ready to launch and use.
manual.disk_access_intro: To completely allow the software to scan and erase your data, you are required to Assign Full Disk Access.
manual.disk_access_macos_new: Steps to Assign Full Disk Access for MacOS:
manual.disk_access_macos_new_note: Applicable for Macs having MacOS 15 Sequoia, MacOS 14 Sonoma and MacOS 13 Ventura.
manual.disk_access_step1: Click Open Full Disk Access button.
manual.disk_access_step2: Full Disk Access window is displayed.
manual.disk_access_step3: Enable the slider displayed against 'D-Secure File Eraser' and enter the administrator password when prompted.
manual.disk_access_step4: A dialog box appears on the screen with a message "D-SecureFileEraser will not have full disk access until it is quit". Click Quit & Reopen.
manual.disk_access_macos_old: Steps to Assign Full Disk Access For MacOS:
manual.disk_access_macos_old_note: Applicable for Macs having MacOS 12 Monterey, MacOS 11 Big Sur.
manual.disk_access_old_step1: Click Open Full Disk Access button.
manual.disk_access_old_step2: Security & Privacy window is displayed. Go to Privacy tab.
manual.disk_access_old_step3: From the bottom, click lock icon and enter your administrator password.
manual.disk_access_old_step4: From the given options on the left pane, select Full Disk Access and mark D-Secure File Eraser.
manual.disk_access_old_step5: A dialog box appears on the screen with a message "D-SecureFileEraser will not have full disk access until it is quit". Click Quit & Reopen.
manual.tabs_buttons_title: Getting Familiar with Tabs and Buttons
manual.tabs_buttons_desc: The D-Secure interface includes tabs for different erasure modes, buttons for actions like 'Erase Now', 'Add Items', and 'Settings'. Familiarize yourself with each element for efficient operation.
manual.info_icon_desc: Click the Info icon in the navbar to view application details and version information.
manual.support_doc_desc: Access support resources and documentation from the navbar.
manual.software_update_desc: Check for and install software updates from the navbar.
manual.help_manual_desc: Open the help manual directly from the navbar for quick reference.
manual.dashboard_view_desc: The main dashboard provides an overview of all erasure features and quick access to key functions.
manual.dashboard_details_desc: The dashboard also displays recent activity, system status, and quick-start options for common erasure tasks.
manual.ordering_know_more: To know more about D-Secure File Eraser, click
manual.ordering_purchase: To purchase the software online, click
manual.ordering_buy_online: Alternatively, click on Buy Online icon on the Menu Bar to purchase the software online.
manual.ordering_select_method: Select either of the methods given above to purchase the software.
manual.ordering_activation_key: Once the order is confirmed, you receive an activation key through email. You require this key to activate the software.
manual.activating_intro: Activate D-Secure with your license key:
manual.activating_cloud_login_desc: Click the key icon in the top menu to open the Activation window. Select "Cloud Activation," enter your Email and Password, and click Login to verify your account status.
manual.activating_online_desc: Select the "Online Activation" option and enter the product key received in your email into the text field. Click the "Activate" button to complete the process, or use "Get it Now" if you do not yet have a license key.
manual.activating_offline_desc: Select the "Offline Activation" radio button to register without an active internet connection. Click "Generate Request Code" to create a code to share with the D-Secure Support Team, then enter the provided Activation Response Code and click "Activate" to finish.
manual.activating_offline2_desc: Offline Activation Select "Offline Activation" and click "Generate Request Code" to create a unique code for the D-Secure Support Team. Once you receive the Activation Response Code from them, enter it into the designated field and click "Activate" to register the software without an internet connection.
manual.activating_offline3_desc: Offline Activation Select "Offline Activation" and click "Generate Request Code" to create a unique code for the D-Secure Support Team. Once generated, the code is automatically copied to your clipboard to be shared for a response. Finally, enter the received Activation Response Code and click "Activate" to finish.
manual.updating_desc: D-Secure automatically checks for updates. You can also manually check via Settings → Check for Updates. Always keep your software updated for the latest features and security patches.
manual.erase_files_intro: To securely erase files and folders:
manual.erase_files_step1_desc: Navigate to the "Erase Files/Folders" tab located in the left sidebar menu. The main interface will display a designated zone labeled "Drag & Drop Files & Folders Here". You can either drag your files directly into this area or click the "Browse Files" link to manually select data from your computer.
manual.erase_files_step2_desc: If you choose to browse manually, a "Select Files or Folders" window will appear. Navigate through your local directories (e.g., C:\dsecure) to find the data you wish to remove. Select the desired file—such as "D-SecureErase.exe"—and click the "Add Selected" button to add it to the erasure queue.
manual.erase_files_step3_desc: The selected items will now appear in the main erasure table. This list provides a detailed overview, including the checkbox to Include the file, the specific File Name, the full system Path, the item Type, and its Size. Confirm all items are correct before proceeding.
manual.erase_files_step4_desc: Once you have verified your selection, click the "Erase Now" button located at the bottom right corner of the window. This action prepares the application to securely destroy the listed data.
manual.erase_files_step5_desc: dialog box will appear as a safety measure. It summarizes the Items selected and the Total files to erase. Review the warning: "This action cannot be undone". If you are certain, click "Yes, Erase" to begin the permanent deletion process.
manual.erase_files_step6_desc: D-SecureErase will now begin shredding the files. You can monitor the progress through the Shredder Animation and the Progress Bar. The interface also displays the real-time file path currently being processed. If necessary, you can halt the operation using the red "Stop" button.
manual.erase_files_step7_desc: Upon completion, a "File's & Folder's Erasure Summary" report is generated. This table confirms the File Path, Size, and shows the final Status as "Erased & Verified". Click the "OK" button to close the summary and return to the main dashboard.
manual.erase_space_intro: Erasing free space removes traces of previously deleted files from your hard drive. This comprehensive process ensures that all remnants of deleted data are permanently removed, preventing any possibility of recovery.
manual.erase_space_select_volume: Select Target Volume Click on the "Erase Volume" tab in the left sidebar to view all available drives. Check the box next to the specific volume you wish to wipe (e.g., C:\ or E:) and click the "Erase Now" button to proceed.
manual.erase_space_locked: Handling Locked Volumes If a drive appears as "Locked / Inaccessible" (e.g., F:\ [Locked]), it cannot be selected for erasure in its current state. Ensure the drive is properly connected and unlocked before attempting to check the selection box for wiping.
manual.erase_space_confirm: Confirm Erasure Once you click "Erase Now," a Confirm Erasure dialog box will appear listing the selected drive (e.g., F:). This action is permanent and cannot be undone; click "Yes, Erase" to proceed with the wiping process or "Cancel" to go back.
manual.erase_space_wipe: Permanently Wipe Selected Volume Confirm the erasure by clicking "Yes, Erase" in the dialog box. This will trigger the wiping process for the selected drive (e.g., F:), permanently removing all stored data to ensure it cannot be recovered.
manual.erase_space_monitor: Monitor Erasure Progress After confirming, the software begins wiping the drive sector by sector. You can track the real-time progress through the percentage bar and the exact sector count being processed (e.g., 20,701,184 sectors). A "Stop" button is available if you need to terminate the process immediately.
manual.erase_space_finalizing: Finalizing Erasure As the process nears completion, the progress bar will reach approximately 88.9%, showing that the vast majority of sectors (e.g., 96,960,512) have been successfully wiped. Keep the software running until the bar reaches 100% to ensure every sector of the selected volume is permanently erased.
manual.erase_space_complete: Completion Notification Once the process reaches 100%, a Volume Erasure information box will appear confirming "Volume erased successfully". Click the "OK" button to close the dialog and return to the main menu.
manual.erase_space_summary: View Erasure Summary After clicking "OK" on the success dialog, a Summary table will appear. This table provides a final log of the operation, including the Target drive (F:), its Capacity (52.00 GB), the total Sectors Erased (109,043,712), and the Method used. Click the blue "OK" button at the bottom right to return to the volume selection screen.
manual.erase_space_reformat: Drive Reformatting After the volume erasure is complete, the drive will appear as unallocated or unrecognized by the system. To make it usable again, right-click the drive in your file explorer and select "Format..." to create a new file system and restore its accessibility.
manual.erase_space_format: Format Volume for Re-use To make the erased drive accessible again, open Windows File Explorer, right-click the wiped volume (e.g., F:), and select "Format...". In the format window, ensure "Quick Format" is selected and click "Start" to restore the drive's file system for normal use.
manual.erase_space_format_done: Formatting Complete Once the quick format is finished, a notification will appear stating "Format Complete". Click the "OK" button to finalize the process; your drive is now fully restored with a clean file system and ready for new data.
manual.volume_select_desc: Select the target volume to erase from the list of available drives.
manual.volume_confirm_desc: Confirm the volume erasure operation before proceeding.
manual.volume_progress_desc: Monitor the volume erasure progress in real-time.
manual.volume_complete_desc: Volume erasure completed successfully with summary details.
manual.deleted_data_select_desc: Choose the drive to scan for deleted data remnants.
manual.deleted_data_scanning_desc: The software scans for deleted data traces on the selected drive.
manual.deleted_data_erasing_desc: Securely erasing all deleted data remnants from the drive.
manual.deleted_data_complete_desc: Deleted data erasure completed with verification summary.
manual.cloud_intro: D-Secure File Eraser allows you to securely erase files stored in your Google Drive cloud storage without needing to open your account manually. This feature ensures complete privacy by permanently removing cloud-stored data.
manual.cloud_step1_note: Note: You must be logged into your Google account for this feature to work.
manual.cloud_step2_desc: Click the "Connect" button to initiate the authorization process. A browser window will automatically open asking you to sign in. Select the Google Account you wish to link with D-Secure to proceed.
manual.cloud_step3_desc: On the consent screen titled "D-Secure wants access to your Google Account," review the access request. Click the "Continue" button to grant the necessary permissions and proceed with the connection
manual.cloud_step4_desc: Once the permissions are granted, the browser will display a confirmation page. Look for the "Success!" header followed by the message "All done. Please go back to D_CLOUDBASE". This confirms that D-Secure has successfully authenticated with your cloud account, and you can now return to the application interface
manual.cloud_step5_desc: Return to the D-SecureErase application. A "Status Update" dialog box will appear confirming that "Google Drive connected Successfully". Click the "OK" button to close the dialog and begin managing your cloud storage
manual.cloud_step6_desc: Select the "Erase Volume" radio button to wipe the entire contents of your cloud storage. The interface will display a "Google Drive - Storage Overview" card showing your current usage statistics (e.g., 18% used, 12.25 GB free). Check the box located on this storage card to select the volume for erasure.
manual.cloud_step6_1_desc: Select the "Erase Files/Folder" radio button to view your cloud directory structure. A file tree will appear showing your Google Drive folders (e.g., "Testing Data"). Expand the folders and check the boxes next to the specific files or subfolders you wish to permanently remove.
manual.cloud_step6_2_desc: Alternatively, select the "Erase Files/Folder" radio button to access individual files instead of wiping the whole volume. The interface will load your cloud directory structure, showing the root folder (e.g., "Google Drive") and subfolders like "Testing Data (1)". This view allows you to navigate through your storage hierarchy to pinpoint specific data for removal.
manual.cloud_step7_desc: Select the "Erase Files/Folder" radio button to browse your cloud directory structure. A hierarchical file tree will appear, displaying your Google Drive folders and files (e.g., "Testing Data", "erase data - Copy"). Expand the directories and check the boxes next to the specific items you want to permanently erase.
manual.cloud_step7_1_desc: Navigate deeper into the directory tree to locate the specific data you intend to destroy. Click the checkboxes next to the individual files (such as .tmp or media files) or entire sub-folders to mark them for deletion. Ensure that the checkboxes are ticked for all items you wish to permanently remove before proceeding.
manual.cloud_step8_desc: After making your selection, click the "Erase Cloud Data" button. A "Confirm Cloud Erasure" dialog box will appear, displaying a summary of the items to be deleted (e.g., "3 files and 1 folders"). The dialog includes a warning that "This action cannot be undone". Click the "Yes, Erase" button to proceed with the permanent deletion of the selected cloud data
manual.cloud_step9_desc: Once confirmed, the application begins the secure erasure process. The interface displays a large shredding animation along with a progress bar and status message (e.g., "Starting cloud erasure...") to keep you informed. If you need to halt the operation immediately, you can click the "Stop" button located at the bottom right of the window.
manual.cloud_step9_1_desc: As the operation advances, the progress bar updates to reflect the percentage of completion (e.g., 33%). The status message below the bar provides specific details about the current action, identifying which file is currently being destroyed (e.g., "Erasing cloud file 1/3: deleted_..."). This allows you to track exactly which data is being processed in real-time.
manual.cloud_step10_desc: When the process completes, the application presents a "Cloud File Erasure Summary" table. This report details the specific files that were processed, showing their full file paths (e.g., ".../erase data - Copy (1)"), file sizes (e.g., 103.09 MB), and their final status as "Erased". Review the details and click the "OK" button to close the summary and finish the operation.
manual.cloud_important_cannot_recover: Erased files are permanently deleted from Google Drive and cannot be recovered
manual.cloud_main_desc: The cloud erase main screen showing available cloud storage options.
manual.cloud_connect_desc: Connect your cloud storage account for erasure operations.
manual.cloud_browse_desc: Browse and select cloud files for erasure.
manual.cloud_select_desc: Select specific cloud items to erase.
manual.cloud_confirm_desc: Review and confirm selected cloud items for erasure.
manual.cloud_processing_desc: Cloud data being processed for secure deletion.
manual.cloud_summary_desc: Cloud erasure operation summary and results.
manual.cloud_volume_overview_desc: Cloud volume storage overview and usage details.
manual.cloud_volume_erasure_desc: Cloud volume erasure confirmation and process.
manual.cloud_final_report_desc: Final cloud erasure report with detailed results.
manual.schedule_intro: Automate erasure tasks by creating schedules. Choose frequency (daily, weekly, monthly), set time, select trace categories, and save the schedule.
manual.schedule_step1_desc: Click on the "Scheduler" icon in the left sidebar to access the configuration screen for automated tasks. This section allows you to create new schedules or manage existing ones to ensure your data is wiped at specific intervals.
manual.schedule_step2_intro: Define the parameters for your automated erasure:
manual.schedule_step2_1_desc: Set how often the task should run (e.g., "Once") and the exact Date and Time for it to start.
manual.schedule_step2_2_desc: Select "New Task" and enter a descriptive Task Name (e.g., "ERASEFILES").
manual.schedule_step2_3_desc: Click "Browse" to choose the files or folders you want to schedule for permanent deletion.
manual.schedule_step3_desc: Click the "Browse" button to open a file selection window. Navigate through your local directories (e.g., F:\Testing Data) and check the boxes next to the specific folders or files you wish to add to the schedule. Click the "Add" button to include these items in your task list.
manual.schedule_step4_desc: Once you have added the items from the browse window, they will appear in the task list showing the Name, Size, and Type. Review the details and click the "OK" button at the bottom right to save and activate your scheduled erasure task.
manual.schedule_step5_desc: Review your finalized task in the list to ensure the correct Name, Size, and Type (e.g., Folder) are displayed. Click the "OK" button to activate the schedule. An "Info" dialog box will appear confirming, "Task 'ERASEFILES' scheduled successfully". Click "OK" on the dialog box to finish the setup.
manual.schedule_step6_desc: After selecting your items, they will be listed in the task table with their Name, Size, and Type (e.g., Folder). manual.preview_report_intro: The Preview Report feature allows you to review erasure details before saving or distributing the report. This ensures accuracy and completeness of all recorded information.
manual.preview_step1_desc: Navigate to the "Reports" tab from the main dashboard. This section displays a chronological list of all erasure operations performed, including the date, time, erasure type (Files/Folders, Free Space, etc.), and status of each operation.
manual.preview_step2_desc: Browse through the list of generated reports and click on the specific report you wish to preview. You can use the search and filter options to quickly locate reports by date range, erasure type, or status. The selected report will be highlighted in the list.
manual.preview_step3_desc: With the report selected, click the "Preview" button located in the toolbar or action panel. This will open a new window displaying the complete report in a formatted, easy-to-read layout.
manual.preview_step4_desc: The preview window shows comprehensive information including erasure summary, list of erased items with their original paths and sizes, erasure algorithm used, verification method applied, timestamp, technician information, and any custom fields or company branding configured in settings. Scroll through all sections to verify accuracy.
manual.preview_screenshot1_desc: The reports dashboard showing all generated erasure reports.
manual.preview_screenshot2_desc: Detailed report preview showing all erasure details and verification results.
manual.save_report_intro: The Save Report feature allows you to store erasure reports locally or export them to various formats for distribution and compliance purposes.
manual.save_step1_desc: Navigate to the "Reports" tab and view the comprehensive list view. Locate the specific report you wish to save based on date, type, or status information.
manual.save_step2_desc: Select the desired report by clicking on it to highlight the entry. Then click the "Save Report" button located in the toolbar.
manual.save_step3_desc: The save dialog presents multiple format options for your report. Select PDF format for professional, universally compatible reports that include digital signatures and cannot be easily modified. PDF reports maintain formatting across all platforms and are ideal for official documentation and compliance requirements.
manual.save_step4_desc: Click the "Browse" button to open the file explorer window. Navigate through your computer's directory structure to select the folder where you want to save the report. You can create a new folder if needed. Choose a location that is easily accessible and properly backed up.
manual.save_step5_desc: In the file name field, enter a descriptive name for your report. The default naming convention typically includes the erasure type, date, and time stamp. You can customize this to match your organization's file naming standards for easier identification and archival.
manual.save_step6_desc: Review all your selections to ensure the format, location, and filename are correct. Click the "Save" button to export the report. A progress indicator will appear briefly and upon successful completion, a confirmation message will display. The saved report is now available at your chosen location and can be shared, printed, or archived as needed.
manual.cloud_report_intro: D-Secure Cloud provides secure encrypted cloud storage for your erasure reports, enabling remote access, centralized management, and automatic synchronization across multiple installations.
manual.cloud_report_step1_desc: Before uploading reports, ensure you are logged into your D-Secure Cloud account. Check the cloud status in the top right corner of the application. If not logged in, click on the cloud icon and enter your D-Secure Cloud credentials (email and password) to authenticate.
manual.cloud_report_step2_desc: Open the Reports tab from the main navigation menu. This shows a comprehensive list of all erasure reports generated by the application, including those already uploaded to cloud (marked with a cloud icon) and those stored locally only.
manual.cloud_report_step3_desc: Review the list and select one or more reports you wish to send to D-Secure Cloud by checking the boxes next to each entry. You can select multiple reports simultaneously for batch upload. Reports already synchronized with cloud will be indicated with a special marker.
manual.cloud_report_step4_desc: With your reports selected, click the "Send to Cloud" button located in the toolbar at the top of the reports interface. This will prepare the selected reports for secure upload to your D-Secure Cloud account.
manual.cloud_report_step5_desc: A confirmation dialog will appear showing the number of reports to be uploaded and estimated upload size. Review this information and click "Yes, Upload" to proceed. The application will begin securely encrypting and transferring the reports to D-Secure Cloud servers.
manual.cloud_report_step6_desc: A progress indicator displays the upload status for each report, showing percentage completed and estimated time remaining. Once upload is complete, a success notification appears, and the reports are marked as synchronized. You can now access these reports from any device by logging into your D-Secure Cloud console.
manual.report_settings_intro: Report settings allow you to customize the appearance and content of erasure reports to match your organization's branding and documentation requirements.
manual.report_settings_step1_desc: Click on the Settings icon (gear/cog icon) located in the top navigation bar or main menu. This opens the comprehensive settings panel where you can configure various aspects of the application's behavior and appearance.
manual.report_settings_step2_desc: Within the Settings panel, locate and click on "Report Settings" from the left sidebar menu. This section contains all customization options related to erasure report generation, formatting, and content.
manual.report_settings_step3_desc: In the Company Details section, enter your organization name, address, phone number, email, and website. This information will appear in the header or footer of all generated reports, providing professional branding and contact information.
manual.report_settings_step4_desc: Click the "Upload Logo" button to select your company logo image file from your computer. Supported formats include PNG, JPG, and SVG. The logo will be displayed prominently on reports. You can adjust the logo size and position using the alignment options provided.
manual.report_settings_step5_desc: Enter the default technician name, title, and digital signature details. You can upload a signature image or create a digital signature. This information certifies who performed the erasure operation and adds authenticity to the report.
manual.report_settings_step6_desc: Enable or disable optional fields such as customer information, device serial numbers, custom notes, or additional metadata fields. Configure which sections should appear in the report and in what order to match your documentation standards.
manual.report_settings_step7_desc: After making all desired changes, click the "Save Settings" button at the bottom of the page. A confirmation message will appear, and all future reports will be generated using these customized settings. You can return to modify these settings at any time.
manual.change_themes_intro: D-Secure File Eraser supports multiple visual themes to enhance user comfort and reduce eye strain during extended use. Choose from Light Mode, Dark Mode, or System Default settings.
manual.themes_step1_desc: Click on the Settings icon (gear/cog) located in the main navigation bar at the top of the application window. This will open the comprehensive settings configuration panel with various customization options.
manual.themes_step2_desc: In the Settings panel, locate and click on "General Settings" or "Appearance" from the left sidebar menu. This section contains interface customization options including theme selection, language preferences, and display settings.
manual.settings_appearance_desc: Customize the application appearance including theme and display preferences.
manual.settings_language_desc: Change the application language to your preferred language option.
manual.erasure_options_intro: Erasure options allow you to configure the default security algorithms, verification methods, and other preferences that control how data is permanently deleted from your system.
manual.erasure_step1_desc: Click on the Settings icon in the main navigation bar to open the settings panel. This centralized location provides access to all application configuration options.
manual.erasure_step2_desc: In the Settings panel, locate and click on "Erasure Options" or "Erasure Settings" from the sidebar menu. This section contains all configuration options related to data erasure algorithms, verification, and security preferences.
manual.erasure_step3_desc: From the "Default Algorithm" dropdown menu, choose your preferred erasure standard. D-Secure offers 20+ internationally recognized algorithms including DoD 5220.22-M (3-pass), DoD 5220.22-M ECE (7-pass), Gutmann (35-pass), NIST 800-88, and others. Higher pass counts provide greater security but require more time.
manual.erasure_step4_desc: Enable the "Verify Erasure" option to ensure data has been completely removed. Select from verification methods such as "Quick Verification", "Full Verification", or "No Verification". Full verification is recommended for sensitive data.
manual.erasure_step5_desc: Configure optional settings such as "Delete files after erasure", "Show confirmation dialogs", "Enable sound notifications", and "Generate automatic reports" to customize the erasure workflow.
manual.erasure_step6_desc: Adjust settings like "Maximum CPU usage" to control how much system resources the erasure process can utilize. Lower settings reduce impact on other applications but extend erasure time. "Process priority" can be set to Low, Normal, or High depending on your requirements.
manual.erasure_step7_desc: After configuring all options, click the "Save Settings" or "Apply" button at the bottom of the panel. These settings will become the default for all future erasure operations. You can modify these settings at any time.
manual.cloud_login_intro: Login to D-Secure Cloud to access cloud-based report management, synchronization, and remote access features. Follow these steps to authenticate your account:
manual.cloud_login_step1: Access the Activation window by clicking the key icon to open the Activation dialog. Select the "Cloud Activation" radio button to use your registered account.
manual.cloud_login_step2: Enter your registered email and password in the provided text fields. The Status field will show "Disconnected" until you successfully log in.
manual.cloud_login_step3: Complete Login. Click the "Login" button to verify your credentials and activate the software via the cloud.
manual.cloud_login_step4: Generate Offline Request. If you choose "Offline Activation," click the "Generate Request Code" button to create a unique machine identifier. A pop-up will confirm that the code has been generated and automatically copied to your clipboard.
manual.cloud_login_step5: Submit and Activate. Share the generated code with the support team to receive your activation response. Once received, paste it into the response field and click the "Activate" button to complete the registration without an internet connection.
manual.faq_q1: 1. What does the demo version of D-Secure File Eraser do?
manual.faq_a1: The demo version of the software only allows viewing traces of files, folders, web browsers, applications or system files. To perform any erasure, you must purchase and register the software.
manual.faq_q2: 2. Why do I need to use D-Secure File Eraser?
manual.faq_a2: When you delete a file from your system, the data is not actually erased. It is just marked as deleted and the space it was using is made available for other files to be stored. This means that the data can still be recovered by using data recovery software. D-Secure File Eraser helps you to securely and permanently erase files, applications and internet traces from your system thereby ensuring your data privacy. Also, the Erase Free Space feature of the software completely erases data in the free space which leaves no possibility of data to be recovered.
manual.faq_q3: 3. Does D-Secure File Eraser remove data beyond recovery?
manual.faq_a3: Yes, D-Secure File Eraser removes data beyond recovery.
manual.faq_q4: 4. Does erasure cause any harm to my hard drive?
manual.faq_a4: Erasing data using D-Secure File Eraser does not cause any harm to the hard drive.
manual.faq_q5: 5. Can I erase a specific file using D-Secure File Eraser?
manual.faq_a5: Yes, you can erase specific files by adding them or dragging the items and dropping them in the software, or you can also erase selected files through the Search & Erase option. For more information, refer to the Erase Files and Folders section.
manual.faq_q6: 6. What if data of the application traces are not erased after the complete erasure process?
manual.faq_a6: It happens in case you have not closed the application before the erasing process or if you are viewing newly created files and folders after the application has restarted on completing an erasure process.
manual.faq_q7: 7. What if the data of System Traces are not erased after the completion of an erasure process?
manual.faq_a7: This problem arises if you view the recently created folders, as the operating system automatically creates some system traces at different time intervals.
manual.faq_q8: 8. What are passes in Erasing algorithms?
manual.faq_a8: Passes in erasing algorithms are the number of times the overwriting process is repeated. For example, in the Peter Gutmann method, a series of 35 patterns is written over the region to be erased.
manual.faq_q9: 9. Which algorithm should I choose for the best erasure?
manual.faq_a9: It depends upon how many times an algorithm overwrites the data precisely, or the number of passes used to erase the data. The more the number of passes used by the algorithm, the more effective it is. Each algorithm performs a different set of features, and you can choose the suitable algorithm as per the requirement. See List of Erasure Algorithms and Their Descriptions to know more.
manual.faq_q10: 10. What if the software is taking too long to erase the data?
manual.faq_a10: It depends on the size of the data you are erasing, the configuration of the system and the algorithm you are using. A large amount of data, lower configuration and more number of passes may consume more time for erasure.
manual.faq_q11: 11. If a single overwrite can erase hard disk securely enough, why are there so many data sanitization algorithms?
manual.faq_a11: Not everyone agrees on a single data sanitization algorithm that can prevent all possible methods of recovering the data. Since many advanced, hardware-based methods of extracting information from hard drives exist, several governmental organizations and researchers have independently devised certain methods of overwriting data that, according to their research, should prevent these advanced recovery methods from working.
manual.faq_q12: 12. Which types of files cannot be erased using D-Secure File Eraser?
manual.faq_a12: System files are crucial files that are used to run your computer. So it is not possible to erase system files.
manual.faq_q13: 13. Is it possible to erase specific internet activities instead of clearing all of them?
manual.faq_a13: Yes, you can choose specific items or internet activities from the list of browsers you want to erase. See Erase Internet Traces to know more.
manual.faq_q14: 14. What if I lose or misplace erasure reports on my system, what can I do?
manual.faq_a14: The application provides functionality to send reports to D-Secure Cloud so they could be secured even when not in use. See Send Report to D-Secure Cloud to know more.
manual.faq_q15: 15. Can I preview and save the erasure reports?
manual.faq_a15: Yes, you can do it. Refer to the Preview and Save sections to know more.
manual.faq_q16: 16. Can I get the reports of the data I have deleted so far?
manual.faq_a16: Yes, D-Secure File Eraser provides erasure reports. See the Reports section for more details.
manual.faq_q17: 17. Can I use D-Secure File Eraser to erase the free space on the primary partition of my hard drive?
manual.faq_a17: Yes, it is secure to use D-Secure File Eraser to erase data in the free space of the hard drive's primary partition as it removes the free space left after deletion of files and folders.
manual.faq_q18: 18. What is the process for scheduling an erase traces task for a specific browser?
manual.faq_a18_p1: To schedule an erasure task for a specific browser, first configure the default settings for that browser in the D-Secure File Eraser settings. After that, you can schedule the erasure task using the schedule erasure tab for the desired duration.
manual.faq_a18_p2: Follow the steps below to schedule an erase traces task for a specific browser:
manual.faq_a18_step1: Click on the Settings icon displayed on the screen.
manual.faq_a18_step2: Select the Erase Traces tab from the Settings window that appears next.
manual.faq_a18_step4: Choose the browser you want to schedule to erase traces and click OK.
manual.faq_a18_step5: Select Schedule Erasure from Select Option displayed on the left pane of the screen.
manual.faq_a18_step6: Select the Erase Traces tab from the right pane of the screen.

manual.themes_dark_mode_desc: Select "Dark Mode" for light text on dark backgrounds. This mode is easier on the eyes in low-light environments.

manual.themes_step3_full_desc: Scroll to the "Theme" or "Appearance Mode" section within General Settings. You will see three available theme options: Light Mode, Dark Mode, and System Default. Each option includes a preview showing how the interface will appear.
manual.themes_light_mode_full_desc: If you prefer a white background, traditional interface with dark text on light backgrounds, select "Light Mode." This mode is ideal for well-lit environments and provides maximum contrast for easy readability during daytime use.
manual.themes_dark_mode_full_desc: Choose "Dark Mode" for a darker color scheme with light text on dark backgrounds. This mode is easier on the eyes in low-light environments, reduces screen brightness, and is preferred by many users for extended work sessions, especially during evening hours.
manual.themes_system_default_full_desc: The "System Default" option automatically synchronizes the application theme with your operating system's theme settings. If your OS is set to Dark Mode, the application will use Dark Mode, and vice versa. This option is convenient for users who want consistency across all applications.

The entire interface will transition to the selected new theme, affecting all windows, dialogs, and panels. Your theme preference is automatically saved and will persist across application sessions.

manual.faq_a18_conclusion: Once you have scheduled the erase traces task for the particular browser, it will run automatically at the designated time and date, erasing all the traces of your browsing history, cookies, and related data. To know more, please refer to the Set Schedule Erasure for Erase Traces section.
manual.faq_q19: 19. Is it possible for the software to automatically erase traces of any files or folder?
manual.faq_a19: Yes, D-Secure File Eraser provides an option to schedule eraser task to perform automatically at predefined period. Refer to the Set Schedule Erasure for Files and Folders section to schedule erasure task automatically.
